file_insights.purpose: DRF permission classes for organization-scoped access control
Export new DRF permission classes and factory functions
changes: Updated module docstring with new usage examples
Phase changed from 'Core Module Models' to 'Authentication & Authorization'

patterns_discovered.description: ViewSet actions (list, create, update, delete) automatically mapped to permission codes using DEFAULT_ACTION_MAP or explicit action_permissions dict
patterns_discovered.benefit: Eliminates repetitive permission setup across similar endpoints

patterns_discovered.description: Permission classes check for organization context via getattr(request, 'organization', None)
patterns_discovered.benefit: Enables tenant-aware permission checking without explicit middleware coupling

patterns_discovered.description: make_organization_permission and similar functions create permission classes dynamically
patterns_discovered.benefit: Reduces boilerplate for simple permission scenarios

patterns_discovered.description: ActionBasedPermission supports 'all' and 'any' operators for combining multiple permission requirements
patterns_discovered.benefit: Flexible permission composition for complex authorization rules

patterns_discovered.description: All permission classes grant immediate access to superusers
patterns_discovered.benefit: Standard Django convention for administrative access

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Missing organization context silently allows access
gotchas_discovered.description: If request.organization is None, check_permission may not properly scope permissions, potentially allowing cross-organization access
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Middleware should ensure organization is always set for authenticated requests

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: Object-level organization mismatch uses string comparison
gotchas_discovered.description: Code converts IDs to strings for comparison: str(obj_org_id) != str(organization.id), which may mask type conversion issues
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Consider using explicit type conversion and logging actual types when mismatch occurs

gotchas_discovered.gotcha: No permission defined for action returns False silently
gotchas_discovered.description: When action_permissions lacks an action mapping, access is denied with only a debug log. Users see generic 'no permission' message
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Could raise a configuration error in development to catch missing permission definitions early

gotchas_discovered.description: Git diff is truncated; full implementation of make_organization_permission, make_platform_permission, make_composite_permission not visible
gotchas_discovered.mitigation: Verify implementation includes proper parameterization and closure handling

approach_outcome.description: Created DRF permission classes for organization-scoped access
key_achievements: Implemented OrganizationScopedPermission with action-based permission mapping
Created factory functions for dynamic permission class generation
Comprehensive docstrings and usage examples provided
Proper integration with existing permissions infrastructure

recommendations.recommendation: Implement middleware to enforce organization context on all authenticated requests
recommendations.rationale: Critical to prevent accidental cross-organization access when organization is None

recommendations.recommendation: Add comprehensive test coverage for edge cases: missing organization, organization mismatch, superuser bypass
recommendations.rationale: Permission classes are security-critical; edge cases must be thoroughly tested

recommendations.recommendation: Document the complete factory function implementations and parameter signatures
recommendations.rationale: Git diff is truncated; developers need clear API documentation for factory functions

recommendations.recommendation: Consider raising ConfigurationError in development when action_permissions is incomplete
recommendations.rationale: Missing permission definitions are likely configuration errors caught too late

recommendations.recommendation: Add more context to permission denial logging (e.g., actual object org vs request org)
recommendations.rationale: String comparison mask type mismatches; explicit logging would improve debuggability
